title: Roast my trading setup
author: BerlinCode42
selftext:
**The Setup**

* init cash: 1000$
* 90% per trade
* 0.05% broker fees
* no SL, no TP, no Hedge, trades at bar closing
* WTI 1H heiki ashi
* from 06 March 2022 to 24 October 2025

**The Result**

* Profit: 49990.93$ (fees already payed)
* Fees: 49190.77$
* Max Drawdown Long/Short: 3.7% / 4.35%
* total Trades Long/Short: 1565 / 1446
* Profit Factor Long/Short: 1.4 / 1.57

**Questions**

1. What can hit this results in real trade conditions? 
2. How high the slippage hits every trade in average?
3. Which broker fits best in your opinion?

comments.author: danni3boi
comments.body: Not sure about the rest but excel isn’t that great of a trading platform.

replies.author: BerlinCode42
replies.body: no excel involved. the table right side is part of the backtest script.

comments.author: IronShirow
comments.body: As far as I understand, you enter after the first color change and exit at the close of the second candle. If that’s correct, the reported performance can’t be accurate, especially when it comes to drawdown.

replies.author: BerlinCode42
replies.body: No you understand it wrong. Green means go long, red go short and white means no forecast\ no trade.

replies.author: BerlinCode42
replies.body: The thesis is quit simple: history is repeating.

replies.author: JoshuaZirkzee11
replies.body: I had a thought about doing something similar with vix pre earnings. Could you point me in the direction of the resources you learned to set something like this up, please?

replies.author: BerlinCode42
replies.body: Oh, thats a lot. I am graduate engineer for computer vision with deeper knowleged about signal processing, feature extraction, ann and ml but also stochastics, analysis and processor architectur. It is not just some papers. But If you are strong in math go for "attention is all you need" paper.

replies.author: JoshuaZirkzee11
replies.body: I studied cs and econ undergrad. thank you, I will check out what you mentioned.

comments.author: emimesa2000
comments.body: Heikin-Ashi candlesticks may show wrong backtesting results, I would highly suggest to try normal candlesticks before going live.

replies.author: BerlinCode42
replies.body: Yes this someone suggested too. But my algo is trading only at bar closing so no lookahead bias intrabar can happen.

replies.author: emimesa2000
replies.body: If so, change the graph to normal candlesticks, the should show you the real statistics behind your strategy.

replies.author: BerlinCode42
replies.body: I am not here to prove something. I am here for the questions above. Dont think this algo becomes public available.

replies.author: emimesa2000
replies.body: My comments are answers to your 1st question.

replies.author: shaonvq
replies.body: You should move on to a new idea, slippage will kill your strat, bro

replies.author: BerlinCode42
replies.body: Thank you for your advice and your effort. There’s no need to repeat your point of view. Have a great day!

comments.author: Mysterious-Joke-2266
comments.body: Everyone's gangster until is real money

comments.author: Aggressive-Net-542
comments.body: I have learned to focus on setups not noise — ChartSnipe 💹

comments.author: jbroskio
comments.body: So you just turn on heikin ashi and trade the trend? Probably won’t work mostly because ha doesn’t really work on lower timeframes.

comments.author: Stock_Spare9728
comments.body: Put money at mouth drop 1k make 49k let's see
